title: Hertha by Algernon Charles Swinburne — Sad Poetry Lines
meaning: "Hertha" by Algernon Charles Swinburne is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 270 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I AM that which began;    Out of me the years roll;...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Algernon Charles Swinburne's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
